channel.title: Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen
channel.description: Recent content in Visual Studio Code on Tue Nguyen

item.title: Cool tricks
item.description: Zen mode Zen mode is distract-free mode that hides away unnecessary detail and allows you focus just on the code. The zen mode can be toggled using command palette. Open command palette: Ctrl + Shift + P Type zen mode and hit Enter Minimap For a large file with many lines of code, you can quickly scroll up and down the file using the minimap on the right side of the editor.

item.title: Extensions
item.description: To install a new extensions, open Extensions by pressing Ctrl + Shift + X, then typing the extension name to search and install. The following are some of my favorite extension that boosts productivity. Prettier This extension (by Prettier) is a code formatter. One of its cool feature is Format on save allowing you to format your code whenever you hit Ctrl + S to save your file. Advanced new file This extension (by patbenatar) allows you to create new files from anywhere in your workspace.

item.title: Getting started
item.description: Installation To install VS Code, following the steps below. Google the keywords download visual studio code, and you will find a link to download the installers Choose the appropriate installer for your operating system (for example, Windows 11, 64-bit) and download Click on the downloaded file and follow the instructions (mostly hit the Next button) After the installation process is completed, you can launch it and start writing code Interface Editor window The editor window is the main part of the editor where you write and edit your code.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: Visual Studio Code (or VS Code) is a free, open-source, lightweight, and cross-platform code editor developed by Microsoft based on the Electron Framework. It is one of the most popular code editors used and recommended by programmers at all levels, from beginners to seasoned professionals. Here are some of the highlighted features. Free. You pay nothing to use all of its features. Cross-platform. VS Code is available for all major operating systems, namely Windows, MacOS, and Integrated terminal.

item.title: Keyboard shortcuts
item.description: Navigation Go to settings: Ctrl + , Go to explorer: Ctrl + Shift + E Go to version control: Ctrl + Shift + G Go to debugger: Ctrl + Shift + D Go to folder find and replace: Ctrl + Shift + F Go to extensions: Ctrl + Shift + X Find and replace In the whole folder Find: Ctrl + Shift + F Replace: click on > of the search box to show the replace box In the current file Find: Ctrl + F Replace: click on > of the search box to show the replace box Command palette Go to command palette: Ctrl + Shift + P Working with multiple files Switch between tabs Forward: Ctrl + Tab Backward: Ctrl + Shift + Tab Search file by name: Ctrl + P Working with one file Go to line in the current file: Ctrl + G Close the file: Ctrl + W Terminal Toggle terminal: Ctrl + ~ Other Copy a line: put the cursor at the line and press Ctrl + C Select a word: put the cursor at the word and press Ctrl + D

item.title: Personalization
item.description: This is my preferred settings for VS Code. Editor Editor font size: 18 px Word wrap: on Word wrap column: 80 characters Themes Best: One Dark Pro Flat Next best: One Dark Pro, Visual Studio Dark Terminals
